items.title: Backend + frontend type-check, unit tests, and convention gate pass
items.precondition: Local checkout on dev/dave after execution
items.expected: All four exit 0; 9/9 org-labels tests pass; no new backend structural violations
items.evidence: tsc BE exit=0 (0 errors); tsc FE exit=0; vitest 9/9 pass; check:conventions --strict exit=0

items.title: Migration backfills null->health and saas->generic; no row left null/saas
steps: 2. SELECT organization_type, COUNT(*) FROM organizations GROUP BY organization_type;
items.expected: Only 'health' and (if any) 'generic' remain — no NULL, no 'saas'
items.notes: Migration 20260623000000 not yet applied on dev — runs at deploy or via npm run db:migrate.

items.title: Admin can create a generic org from the Mission Control popup and change type later
items.precondition: Logged in as admin; on /admin Mission Control
steps: 2. Set Type = Generic, fill required fields, submit
3. Open the org detail; change Type Health<->Generic
items.expected: Org is created with organization_type='generic'; type switches without a 409 'cannot be changed' error
items.notes: Create-popup selector + relaxed guard are code-complete and tsc-verified; not click-tested in-browser (QA used the existing generic org 6, and the backend create path was exercised earlier). The setOrganizationType 409 immutable guard is removed in code.

items.title: Generic org dashboard shows generic labels; health org unchanged
items.precondition: One generic org and one health org, each with a logged-in user
steps: 1. Log in as the generic org; visit sidebar, /dashboard, /pmsStatistics, journey insights
2. Confirm Business Hub, Revenue Hub, Direct/Partner/Total Leads, Revenue, Lead Sources/Trends, Customer Journey
3. Log in as a health org; confirm Practice Hub, Referrals Hub, Self/Doctor/Total Referrals, Production are unchanged
items.expected: Generic surfaces read non-healthcare; health surfaces are byte-identical to before
items.evidence: Local browser QA (org 6 DentalEMR=generic): /dashboard shows Business Hub, Revenue Hub, 'Revenue · Year to date', Leads; /pmsStatistics shows Revenue Hub, 'Revenue & leads', 'Leads · {month}', Leads timeframe, Top sources. Health (org 8) returns organizationType=health (curl) and the label map's health values equal the original strings (unit-tested), so health renders unchanged. Browser QA also caught + fixed ProductionPanel and ~15 undercounted PMS/Revenue-Hub labels.
items.notes: Verified generic in-browser. Follow-up (not swapped): onboarding wizard copy, ReferralMatrices loading phrases, api/pms/mapping default labels, a few self/walk-in legend words. Health-unchanged confirmed logically (not screenshotted — MCP browser was flaky on this machine).

items.title: Client-facing agent output reads generic for a generic org; health identical
items.precondition: Generic org with data; ability to run monthly + GBP agents
steps: 1. Run the monthly pipeline (Summary, ReferralEngine, Opportunity) for a generic org
2. Run GBP review-reply + local-post drafts for a generic org
items.expected: Generic output uses customer/business/lead/revenue/you; output still parses to valid JSON; health output unchanged
items.evidence: Direct LLM run (claude-sonnet-4-6) of ReferralEngineAnalysis with a sample PMS payload: generic output used leads/revenue/business/partner/customer/owner throughout (1 residual 'referral' vs 5+ before the {{vocab_directive}} was added); health output unchanged (referrals/production/doctor). parsed_ok=true for both. No email path touched.
items.notes: Generation proven clean. Stored dashboard cards for existing orgs still show pre-change wording until the next normal agent run — the orchestrator/email path was deliberately NOT invoked to guarantee zero emails.

items.title: Generic scheduled ranking does not surface dental specialties/competitors
items.precondition: Generic org with a GBP-linked location; ranking run
steps: 1. Trigger a scheduled ranking run for a generic org
2. Inspect the identified specialty/category and competitor set
items.expected: Specialty is a general business category (not 'orthodontist'); competitors are not dental
items.notes: Live scheduled path wired via work.organizationId. Competitor-onboarding (first-time) path still defaults to health until re-identified on the next scheduled run — documented follow-up.

items.title: Jo copy review in a staging generic account
steps: 1. Jo clicks through every hub and agent output
2. Records any healthcare wording that remains
items.expected: Jo signs off that client-facing copy reads non-healthcare
